reviews.review_title: Games fun, have a major issue
reviews.review_description: This game is fun , it actually helped me with irl football and my hands are way better. My issue is I have to keep deleting it because my movements like picking up the helmet are delayed. When I release the ball ingame it doesnt throw until after my ball stops moving. I get one playthrough and then it just becomes a cycle. If I can get help or this gets fixed this game would be tons of fun. Also I’d like to play defense on exhibition if thats not too much work.

developer_response.body: Hey There!  it sounds like this issue is one with the headset itself.  I'd be curious if the firmware is up to date, and if your headset is running out of space?

reviews.review_description: Once you get the throwing down and catching it's super fun and idk what people are talking about I've ran the ball 30+ yards for a td off the throw

developer_response.body: Hey there!  Really, it's all about the throwing - but if you want to run the ball you have to set up a play that will distract defenders long enough to allow for those big plays.  Sorry it didn't meet your expectations!

reviews.review_title: games fun but could be better
reviews.review_description: the game would be better if you could run faster so you could actually make touchdowns without throwing it every 2 seconds and you should be able to switch positions like wide receiver or running back etc.

developer_response.body: Hey! Appreciate the feedback, you don't have to throw every 2 seconds, you just have to make your pass while you're still in the pocket.  The game IS a QB simulator after all :). though you can absolutely run the ball, it's a practiced skill that you will need to cultivate with time :)

reviews.review_description: This is the one vr game that genuinely gets me hype, every other game is boring overtime.

developer_response.body: Hey Cutlerward70! Thanks for the kind word, we really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description:
No updates just know that and no refunds
Regret is all I feel when I look at this game ￼ 
Just a cash grab

developer_response.body: Bruh.  The game was released 6 years ago and received countless free updates.  What you are playing now is the *complete* version of the game.  I'm sorry you came in with the wrong expectations, but you're talking about a budget indie title, self funded, that expanded from 300 mb to 2 GB with free add-ons over the course of three headset generations (Quest, Quest 2 And Quest 3).  I don't say this often, but for crying out loud, cut us a little slack.  Cash grab? please.

reviews.review_description: I absolutely LOVE this game but I think if the ball is thrown out of bounds you should still be able to run for it (for the people that can jump) and a defense mode would be fun too!

developer_response.body: Hey TTVPlucky1874!  Thanks for the kind word!  the out of bounds idea isn't a bad one, though we have to be careful as going out of bounds can make the AI act a little wonky if the ball is still in play.

reviews.review_description: The game mechanics are great. Probably the closest you can get you having control of an offense/ball placement and the ability to run/configure plays. The problem is it’s half baked. Some very fun modes like exhibition become stale because you can’t change the length of the game, go for 2, or overtime. Basically if you’re losing by two scores with 5 minutes left. Good luck, no comeback without the CPU help. No knock on the Devs, i understand they moved to the next game and would need the required funding to update this game or create a sequel. Still worth your time, especially in the beginning. If there is a creator fund for this game or anyway the player base can help the Devs, they should let us know! We want more of this game!

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  

Heh, I'd clarify that the game is definitely full-baked, but some expectations might be geared toward a larger experience whereas 2MD is at its heart a QB arcade game.  (even the name stands for "2 minute drill" which was the entire game at one point!).  FWIW you can change the length of Exibition games in the practice field, but I hear ya on the other stuff.  

I would just say that all of the choices (even the weird ones) were definitely deliberate and were based on a great deal of player feedback, and since launching the game has had a ton of updates (it is definitely getting along in years since it released in 2020!)

I also think we should be able to be on defence to make it more fun., and I don't like how it's to easy for the defense to tackle u and I wish we could truck and that's it.

reviews.review_title: Great game but I have some suggestions.
reviews.review_description:
I love this game, I’ve had it for almost 3 years. (I got it Christmas of 2022) I’ve played it consistently through the years and I’ve always wanted to write a review. I highly would recommend this game and I have to alot of people I meet and have added on vr. 

Suggestions:
1.I’d love to see some for of defending this game,. Whenever I’m playing exhibition or anything else. I always wonder what it would be like to play against another quarterback 
2. I wanna know if there would be any chance of multiplayer? Idk thought it’d be cool to suggest.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  Glad you've gotten a good time out of the game since '22! 

I hear you (and others) on defense.  it's one of those things that, unfortunately, had we had the foresight like 7 years ago when first designing the game we could have built into the engine, but at this juncture it would be like trying to bolt a sidecar on a pogo stick: the foundation that's there is really focused on one thing (offensive play with the player QB at the center of the action) and with the tools at our disposal making defense fun and compelling would require a mountain of work (and money lol).

reviews.review_description: Terrible game. do not buy. Mechanics are completely unrealistic. Playbook is tedious, tackling and CPU defense is completely awful. Animations terrible. Not to mention throwing mechanics don’t work half the time. The same throw will end up in completely different places.

developer_response.body:
Respectfully, while I’m disappointed you did not have a good experience, it seems many of your issues may be the result of poor hardware or setup. 2MD’s mechanics have been tweaked for the last 5 years and functionally are the most accurate you’ll get. (We literally have videos of kids as young as 6 or 7 years old nailing passes consistently 🙂)

So my first suggestion would be to stop and look at your setup. Make sure your headset is updated and your controllers are fully charged. You will have consistent passes more than 95% of the time. 

Secondly, with regards to the playbook - it’s actually super simple and easy to design a multitude of plays,  but admittedly it won’t hold your hand if that’s what you’re looking for. Honestly tho, most players jump in immediately without issue tbh. 

Animations are over the top because, let’s be honest now, this is an over-the top arcade game. I’m a little confused how you could see the store page, videos, and multitude of reviews on the internet but still be surprised that we have such a presentation?

Anyway, while I’m again disappointed you didn’t dig the game, I do sincerely hope that in case you didn’t get a refund you give my suggestions a try. Take care.

reviews.review_description: The game is AMAZING! The designs are great and the chaotic nature of the game is amazing. My only complaint of it is the CPU. This one time I caught the ball as a receiver and am running down the field to the endzone. Then a receiver runs at the speed of light and tackles me immediately. Devs maybe tone down the AI for the defense. Other than that the rest of the game is really good. Your hard work is very appreciated by the community!

developer_response.body: Hey there!  So defensive players have a set max speed that doesn't really change.  the key here is ensuring you're open not just for the catch, but also to be able to gain additional yardage - just like in real football, coverage matters.  I strongly encourage you to write plays with this in mind - create openings, or have additional offensive players cover your intended receiver as they will automatically do so once you catch the ball.

reviews.review_description: I have played this game since its release and still till this day, I really hope you guys get sponsorships or some sort of funding and they game can reach it’s maximum potential. I’m not saying it’s a bad game it is a great game in my opinion!

developer_response.body: Appreciate the kind word!  We've paused active development at this time but we've left the door open for more just in case. :)

Hey FluffyHedgehog9389!  

I would say that this is probably the most frequently requested feature addition, unfortunately it would be the hardest to implement without significantly retooling the foundation of the game, as such it's not really feasible for us.  I think we decided, however, that if we ever get funding to make a sequel, we absolutely would make defense part of the experience from the get-go.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind word!  Honestly we felt like we were at a good point in terms of content, but I haven't shut the door entirely

reviews.review_description:
This game is honestly the best football game on VR as of right now, but I think there could be a way this could be 10 times better, by adding defensive gameplay. Now, before you say it's too hard to do, keep in mind NFL Pro Era 25 has done this before and executed it extremely well. Here is my proposal to add defensive gameplay to 2MD VR Football:
1. Configure some button (like A or X) on defense that allows you to switch to the player closest to the ball carrier/ball.
2. With another button on the controller, let's say Y for this example, make it so that holding down that button opens up a menu of plays, which you can then use your opposite hand to readily select and adjust before the snap.
3. Allow for things like adjusting the personnel on field during a given play, however this cannot be adjusted on field during before snap.
4. To get past linemen, push them away in a manner similar to the stiff arm function, the spin move can also be used to get past them.
5. Use stiff arm motion to hit tackle, and use the triggers to wrap tackle. Hit tackles if missed, should eat away around 45% of your speed for a few seconds, to avoid abusing it and forcing a fumble nearly every play.
6. 3 stops in a row gives you a hot streak, where you will have increased speed and fumble chance on both kinds of tackles increases
7. With the marker function that designs plays, add an option that makes the marker lines either end in an arrow for blitz assignments, a straight line that ends where a receiver is for man coverage, and a large oval/circle of adjustable size for zone coverage 
8. To help defense gameplay not just be blind guessing of what play, offenses should have some preset formations (like I-formation, trips, goalline, etc.), and before the play there should be a menu saying how much of each position there is (TE/RB/WR), so the defense can anticipate what the play will be.
Please try and implement this, it would be a dream come true if you considered putting this in the game!

developer_response.body:
Hey RepublicoftheSudan! thanks for the kind word, and for taking the time to provide your recommendations - the issue we tend to run into with 2MD vs pro era, is that 2MD's foundation was strictly built around offensive play and the QB only.  We didn't have the necessary pieces in place to allow for easy modification of the model in VR.  

We *did* do a defensive mode for the PSVR version of the original 2MD, this was done by allowing player two to hijack the defensive line using a controller, but it was done outside of VR using an overhead viewpoint.

We actually do have concepts in place for possible defensive modes, however the changes are so extensive it's not really feasible to tack onto the original game (which is honestly pushing 6 years old now, with base code that we first developed way back in 2017 if you can believe it).  Again, it's because the foundation of physics/interaction/movement/AI of our current engine is built fundamentally around the QB

At the end of the day, we decided that we'd want to build a whole new game from the ground up incorporating the best from 2MD, taking into account the years of gameplay and feedback we've gotten from our players.  the project would amount to something much more expensive for us than anything we've embarked on before, and finding publishers willing to invest in an unlicensed VR football game was, unfortunately an  insurmountable challenge.  We have left the door open however, should one decide to take the plunge with us!

But I do encourage you to continue to explore the modes and features the game does have to offer, as it may scratch some of those itches!

reviews.review_description: Been playing this game for years and always ones I can rely on. From what I’ve read the game is kind of at its full state and won’t update much anymore which is perfectly fine. Really hope they can get some kind of funding for a new version of this game. Really creative creators and good community suggestions I think could make an even better version of this game. Still personally think this is the best football game on the market. I have both that are available and still have yet to be impressed by “the other” football game. As for the devs, keep those ideas rolling because you never know when you could get the funding to make another amazing football game all us fans would love. This was my first VR game and was never Dissapointed once (except the many times I lost in the last round and not win championship😂) but seriously, thank you guys for making such a fun and unique football game for us all to play. And I hope to see a new one in the future!

developer_response.body: Hey Dclutch20! Thanks for the kind word and the understanding.  We're game to make a bigger, better 2MD if we're ever approached by any publisher willing to fund the endeavor.  Since we're not tied to a license, it gives us the chance to try creative ideas and sometimes experiement with off-the wall concepts and gameplay. Until then, however, we'll remain the steward of 2MD for as long as there's a platform to play it on :)

reviews.review_description: I see that the game doesn’t get updated like at all but you guys reply to reviews are yall still working on the game or what game is still great though I see as there is like wasted potential

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  

Heh, I would assume that replying to reviews such as yours would be seen as a good thing, right?  I want it to be clear to folks that 2MD as a game is "complete" and not simply abandonware.  We still actively push small updates as necessary to different quest platforms (mostly legacy hardware stuff) and fix backend issues to make sure the game is compliant with the most recent Horizon OS, and runs smoothly for all our players.  That's what continuous support looks like.

And, to be clear, 2MD on quest is over *Five* years old at this point.  There are few examples of games that aren't games-as-a-service or loaded with predatory micro transaction content that will pull off that kind of continual support :) .  Honestly, the game got something like 150 updates in the first 3 years of its presence on the storefront (new modes, teams, leagues, full games, stats, bonus content, etc) all for free for all players without any change in price.  I think it's expanded from the original APK size of 350 mb to something like 2 GB total.

2MD is old enough that any bigger updates / changes would require a big change in the engine and a ton of work (and cost) to implement, which kind of makes little sense at this point as it's really been refined from the ground up to be a QB-centric experience.  Adding receiver mode was doable in that sense, but doing other positions or modes would unfortunately not be possible without significant funding, and given the alternative on the storefront is a fully licensed NFL title we don't really have the kind of mojo to get that support ;_; .

Regardless, I want to be clear, I appreciate your review, and I appreciate the question!

reviews.review_description:
I would love to see a few things added.
- Uniform customization be a little better like each piece can be color changed
- Maybe number changing? Not really necessary but would be cool to have further customization.
I’m trying to stay realistic so I’m not adding too much.
Edit: Also user player speed is 2 seconds per 10 yards which feels very slow. I feel like 10 speed players should be 4.3-4.2 40 speed. It makes explosive plays almost impossible on the ground.

Thanks!

developer_response.body: Hey Chodaskoo!  Thanks for the kind word and the suggestions.  Honestly I think big additions probably aren't in the cards at this point since we updated the game pretty regularly for 3-4 years and hit a steady state where it's the only football game compatible with every Quest headset, kind of leaving us doing occasional performance and stability updates at this point.  I think, if we were ever to get funding, we'd want to do an entirely new game from scratch that incorporates all the wonderful user suggestions we've gotten thus far.

developer_response.body: Hey SuuSolo! thanks for the kind word!

reviews.review_description: Good

developer_response.body: Hey ieatpig, thanks for the kind word! I appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: you need to have a update it’s been 3 years

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  Actually, while our last update was a little more than a couple years ago (Quest 3 and 3s enhancements), the game itself is actually 5 years old, and had over 150 updates in the initial 3 year period that expanded it to over4 times its original size :)

I like to say it's like we built a car out of a bicycle, adding as many features and tons of content as we could, all for free, without ever raising the base price.

Unfortunately you can only do that for so long - and while other companies justify their continued work with predatory practices like paid content and games-as-a-service, we just don't ever want to go down that route.  So, we reached a point where we felt the game is really a total package, complete and stable, and decided to put further updates on pause.  But, as you can see, we're still actively supporting / patching any issues that may arise (mostly it's legacy headset stuff, like Quest 2 and even Quest 1 which we still support) and answer questions from kindly players such as yourself :)

Sorry to hear you had some difficulty with the control - it's actually dependent upon your setting and how you're playing (if you're playing with manual receiver mode on, you can tweak how your controls are oriented when you catch the ball).  check out the videos in the locker room for details, and there's detailed setting on the control panel in the practice area as well!

reviews.review_title: devs can you please fix this glitch,

developer_response.body:
Hey CJ10K!  Thanks for the kind word!

That's a supremely odd glitch - we've never encountered a hard freeze like that.  I would suspect your copy has been corrupted, so I'd actually suggest deleting and reinstalling the game, followed by a full power cycle of the headset.

Hey.  So.  we don't control refunds.  We also updated the game continuously for like 4 years (it was originally 350 mb at launch, and we added 1.5 gigs of content to it, tons of new game modes, etc, all for free without charging anyone a dime).  We've since moved on, but at no point did we say we will never update it again if something meaningful and cool is possible. 

I'm sorry you didn't enjoy the game, if you still want a refund I think the best you could do would be to email Meta directly?

reviews.review_description: good game and all but i don't like the movement, is there a way to made the movement headset oreianted

developer_response.body: Hey connor710!  unfortunately headset-driven motion doesn't really work for football, as you have to be able to look independently of where you are going (especially when avoiding defenders) though there are different movement styles available to you when you are playing as receiver if that helps!

reviews.review_title: Meh, its an alright game but not great
reviews.review_description: 2md football has some excellent and fun features like, you can customize your own plays and customize your teams uniform colors. That is the only parts that I like about this game. But when it comes to the throwing, its really not that good. I have been playing this game for a while and analyzed every throw i did while playing in games. The throwing seems inconsistent if you are just releasing it good. The release for me looks just fine but the ball will either spike down to the ground or just launch it to the sky. I suggest the devs to make the throwing assisted to make it easier and have better results. I would give this game a 2.5 And note that i am a football athlete so i know that the throwing is really unrealistic. Conclusion: The game is fun but the throwing needs some fixing

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  Thanks for the feedback, though unfortunately I don't think the throwing behaviors you're describing represents what most players should be experiencing.

Our throwing model is actually one of the best you'll find in VR - if you're having issues, I suspect you might be dealing with a number of factors.  First and foremost, it's not just about the release: the windup makes a difference as well. Please avoid holding your hand behind your head as this can result in poor tracking of the controller.  Second, make sure you're not standing in front of a window or other source of bright light, as this can also impede good tracking.  lastly, be sure your controllers are well charged and your firmwares are up to date.

Your experience as you've described it is absolutely not the norm, and I'd suggest you reach out to us at contact@truantpixel.com if the above fixes don't remedy the situation.

reviews.review_description:
EDIT: it has been like 2-3 years now and not a single update. What happened? There is so much that could be added into this game whether it’s small add ons or whatever but they just left the game to die :(


This game has everything you want: throwing the ball, catching, run plays, you can do field goals, and punt. You can do full 60 minute games or 2 minute games and try to win the champ. You customize everything including the plays. Only thing I wish the game had is a full season mode and more teams. If they had that this game would be a 6 out of 5 stars!!!

developer_response.body:
Hey 0mni.Man!  

We dropped free updates for like 4 years (game released in 2020, our last update was for Quest 3 launch).  We're a tiny team of 3, and we can't really update / add content ad -infinitum :)  FWIW, the game used to just be tackle dummies, and we went on and added realistic players, fantasy players, leagues, many, many more stadiums, exhibition modes, practice modes, receiver modes, and more!  I'm honestly proud of the fact we were able to expand the game from 300mb to nearly 2GB in content, all for free!  But we had to put a pause on further updates while we develop other games.  I hope you understand :)

reviews.review_description: This game sucked o line is to big to see over mechanics suck wr sucks so not reccomend refunded it immediately

developer_response.body:
Hey bud.  Gonna say, giving us a 1 star review after you refunded the game is extremely questionable, but to answer your complaints, you seem to have missed the height adjustment slider in the settings area to let you see over the offensive line.

U Need a chill indeed :/

reviews.review_description: My favorite football vr game 10\10

developer_response.body: Hey koby6366, thanks for the kind word! it's appreciated!

reviews.review_title: An Underrated Gem in VR Sports Gaming.
reviews.review_description:
2MD: Football Unleashed All Star stands as a great example of how in VR, quality gameplay and refined design can outshine big budget production. Despite being developed by a smaller studio, the game delivers an impressive array of mechanics and a satisfying amount of content.

Performance wise, it runs smoothly even on older hardware like the Quest 2. In my experience, I haven’t encountered any crashes or bugs, which speaks to the developers attention to optimization and stability.

My only significant critique is the lack of recent major updates. While it's understandable that the initial hype has faded, introducing new features and content could turn the already amazing experience into something exceptional.

Overall, 2MD: Football Unleashed All Star is one of the most enjoyable VR football games/sports games available. In my opinion, it could compete with more prominent titles like NFL Pro Era if it was still actively being developed. Despite this the game still offers a uniquely engaging and arcadey take on virtual football.

developer_response.body:
Hey LankBan!  Thanks for the kind word, we really appreciate it!

So, as far as updates - would you believe the game is pushing 5 years old?  We actually did more than 20 major updates for the first 3 years, but had to slow down as the platform shifted from Quest 1 to Quest 2 to Quest 3;  we did push out a major performance enhancement update for Quest 3, but since then we've avoided doing much more as it would require major changes to the engine and currently we're the *only* football game on the platform that supports all Quest headsets.

So it's not really a lack of player hype, honestly its more a desire to make sure everyone is having the same high-quality stable and consistent experience. :)

reviews.review_description: can not throw more than a inch and the npcs can not catch at ALL do not buy waste of money

developer_response.body: Sorry you had a bad experience, but I suspect the issue is with either your controllers or your setup.  take a look at the helpful videos we've placed in the locker room to teach you how to best throw the ball, avoid things like holding your hand behind your head, and please make sure you controllers are adequately charged.

reviews.review_description: This is the best football game on VR. Just add a multiplayer option. Please. This game is soo fun. The game play and controls are great. Please keep updating this game.

developer_response.body: Hey Retired_Crypto!  Thanks for the kind word!  MP would be a pretty huge undertaking (the core game honestly is not configured for it).  We haven't ruled out more updates in the future, should the inspiration strike!

reviews.review_description: So fun because putting the catching on manual is overpowered love the receiver mode I lover the 2 min drills and please make a update where you can sometimes break the tackle and even if you originally got tackled after the catch it still lets you run because running after the catch is so satisfying but I love this game

I love this game, but there’s just two things,

1.one glitch I encountered is when you win a championship in full exhibition mode it doesn’t add to your championship awards (this one is less important though)

2. I know you guys have moved on to a different game and you guys don’t have many funds for this game but I’d love to see things such as team huddles, more team color customization , being able to select your jersey name and number, and lastly an option to have arms (and maybe a full body)

This game is amazing and I’d hate to see it die down and never have another update.

developer_response.body:
Hey RevolverPerson_00!  Thanks for the kind word! to address your notes:

1. Exhibition mode doesn't count toward the championship since the difficulty is under the player's control.  it's quite literally exhibition, hence it does not count as an arcade challenge which is what the championship tracks.

2. I'd love to add more stuff if we could get a publisher willing to fund it!  I'm talking defense, online play, the whole 9 yards.

take care!

reviews.review_description: When your a QB every defence dude are blocking your view to throw to a receiver often just hitting the ground but some time it can be a great experience  of football but I think pro era football is better but still great game.

developer_response.body: Hey there! if you're having trouble seeing over the defenders, I suggest adjusting your height using the slider in the practice field :) that should solve your issue and hopefully elevate the experience!

reviews.review_description: This is my favorite game to play after a long day at work, or if I’m feeling upset or stressed or have energy building up inside, I play this game every time and it’s an absolute stress reliever! It’s addicting to play too, this game will make you feel like your either a Mahomes, Allen, Jackson, etc, feel like a QB! This is best for those that are in need of offseason QB skills workouts! Highly recommend this! Best VR football game to play! They could definitely add a defensive mode. There should be some actual tackling like physics engine could use a bit of an upgrade. That’s really about it.

reviews.review_description: Hard for my son to see over the linemen to throw. Once the defender touches anyone it’s a tackle so no kicking or other moves you may be used to from madden type games.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

There’s actually a height adjustment in the practice field, so as long as your son is at least 4 ft tall he can be more than 6 feet tall in the game!

And while there is no ‘kicking’ you can throw punts and field goals in exhibition mode!

reviews.review_title: Don't buy It's not worth it.
reviews.review_description: The defense is too fast.

developer_response.body: Sorry you didn't dig it.  I'm glad to say you're in the minority in terms of opinion - the defense "isn't" too fast though, I think you're likely moving out of the pocket too quickly or trying to run the ball too far.  Football is a game of inches most of the time, and 2MD is really about executing a strong play and accurate passing.  if you read this, please consider trying again, or reach out to us for helpful tips in order to get the most out of the experience!
